name: Shrinivasa
dates_notes: Post-Nimbārka by guru-paramparā; dated by manuscript chronology.
core_thesis: Śrīnivāsa preserves Nimbārka's *svābhāvika-bhedābheda* without doctrinal innovation, his role being principally architectural: to make the school's terse founding bhāṣya philosophically navigable. He affirms the simultaneous intrinsicality of *bheda* and *abheda*; the threefold reality of *cit*, *acit*, and *Īśvara* (Kṛṣṇa with Rādhā); the *jīva* as eternally an *aṃśa*; and *bhakti–prapatti* as the operative soteriology. Where his work is decisive is in supplying the *pūrvapakṣa–uttarapakṣa* structure absent from Nimbārka's compressed *sūtra*-glosses: he stages and answers Advaitic and Bhāskarīya objections that the *Pārijāta-Saurabha* leaves implicit, thereby making *svābhāvika-bhedābheda* defensible as a developed Vedāntic position rather than a doctrinal slogan. The Sanaka-sampradāya treats his reading as canonical, and post-Śrīnivāsa Nimbārka commentators (Keśava-Kāśmīrī, the *Kaustubha-Prabhā* tradition) build on his exegetical scaffolding rather than returning directly to Nimbārka's own text.

engaged_works.title: Vedanta-Kaustubha
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Nimbārka's Pārijāta-Saurabha.
engaged_works.summary: The *Vedānta-Kaustubha* is Śrīnivāsa's *vyākhyā* on Nimbārka's *Vedānta-Pārijāta-Saurabha* and the canonical sub-commentary of the Sanaka-sampradāya. Where Nimbārka's bhāṣya is famously terse, the *Kaustubha* opens out the doctrinal *prameyas*: it explicates the *svābhāvika* — intrinsic, unconditioned — character of both *bheda* and *abheda* between Brahman, *cit*, and *acit*; it stages the *pūrvapakṣa* against *māyā-vāda* and *aupādhika-bhedābheda* and supplies the *uttarapakṣa* the founding bhāṣya only gestures at; and it elaborates the *aṃśa-aṃśin* relation by which *jīvas* are eternally portion-and-whole of Brahman without merger or severance. The work also clarifies the school's Vaiṣṇava theological commitments — Kṛṣṇa with Rādhā as *parabrahman*, *prapatti* and *bhakti* as the means of liberation — and supplies the structural template that Keśava-Kāśmīrī's *Kaustubha-Prabhā* will subsequently sub-comment in turn. It is, in effect, the operative doctrinal text of the Nimbārka-line tradition.
